title: QUIC client-side 0-RTT rejection cleanup work item
summary: Add the rejected-resumption cleanup branch that discards dormant ZeroRtt packet-protection material while leaving accepted attempts intact and keeping anti-replay support out of scope.
completion_notes: The managed client/runtime path now discards dormant ZeroRtt packet-protection material when the PSK-capable branch is rejected at ServerHello, keeps the accepted branch intact, and leaves receive-side replay controls, key update, transfer, retry, and public API widening out of scope.
verification_plan: Run focused requirement-home tests for the direct bridge-state cleanup helper, the runtime rejected-branch cleanup, the runtime accepted-branch retention case, the randomized cleanup boundary, and the public no-broad-support guard; run the permanent BenchmarkDotNet suite for the rejected-vs-accepted cleanup branch; then run SpecTrace render and validation checks.
out_of_scope: opportunistic refactors unrelated to the rejected-resumption cleanup branch
